title: RPAS‑CM‑GRA‑001
description: RPAS Governance Guardrails and Cloud‑Ready Criteria — Authoritative machine‑readable encoding.

G1.title: Authority Boundary
G1.principle: AI proposes, humans decide, orchestrator executes.
rules: AI may only provide advisory output.
AI must never apply changes, approve changes, or bypass governance rituals.
All state‑changing actions require explicit human authorization.
The Orchestrator (C# layer) is the only execution authority.
G1.purpose: Prevent unauthorized or implicit mutation of state.

G2.title: Lifecycle Integrity
G2.principle: Every state transition must follow the ritual sequence.
rules: No skipping steps.
No fast‑tracking.
No retroactive mutation.
Every artifact must reflect its correct stage in the lifecycle.
G2.purpose: Protect governance flow and prevent corruption of process.

G3.title: Evidence & Lineage
G3.principle: Every artifact must be fully traceable.
rules: RTM entries, amendments, decisions, and CSR versions must be append‑only.
No in‑place edits to governed artifacts.
All history is preserved.
G3.purpose: Ensure immutability, auditability, and reconstruction of truth.

G4.title: Determinism
G4.principle: Execution must be predictable, idempotent, and replay‑safe.
rules: No nondeterministic side effects.
No implicit writes.
No state mutation outside Execution rituals.
No replays causing double‑writes.
CSR stamping happens exactly once per execution.
G4.purpose: Prevent divergence between intended and actual system state.

G5.title: Read vs Act
G5.principle: Experience tiers may observe, visualize, analyze, and advise — but only the Orchestrator may act.
rules: Next.js Researcher Dashboard is advisory‑only.
Blazor Governor Portal approves/executes, but cannot propose on behalf of users.
AI never performs execution.
No UI may bypass rituals and directly mutate the ledger.
No endpoint may accept writes without ritual context.
G5.purpose: Enforce strict separation between view, advice, and authority.

CR1.title: Deterministic Execution
CR1.requirement: All rituals are idempotent and safe for distributed retries.

CR2.title: Authority‑Gated Mutation
CR2.requirement: No mutation occurs without explicit human‑attributed decision artifacts.

CR3.title: Append‑Only History
CR3.requirement: All superseded state remains queryable. History is never rewritten.

CR4.title: Failure Safety
CR4.requirement: System remains safe under restarts, retry storms, and multi‑region duplication.

CR5.title: Experience Decoupling
CR5.requirement: The UI is replaceable. Governance must live in the core, not in the interface.

intelligence.name: Intelligence Tier
intelligence.output_contract: Advisory JSON (suggestedType, proposedDescription, justification, confidence, metadata)

experience_researcher.name: Experience Tier — Researcher Dashboard
experience_researcher.constraint: Draft amendments only; no execution authority

data.name: Data Tier
data.constraint: Append‑only storage for RTM, Amendments, and CSR stamps

validation_gates.name: Mechanical Integrity
validation_gates.validates: Only declared files modified, no untracked artifacts

validation_gates.name: Build Integrity
validation_gates.validates: Full compilation, dependency resolution, Aspire mesh health

validation_gates.name: Topology Check
validation_gates.validates: Project structure, component graph, service mesh consistency
